LP_SOUNDTRACK_PROGRESS_ROUTINE

The LP_SOUNDTRACK_PROGRESS_ROUTINE function is an application-defined callback function used with the XAddSongToSoundtrack function. It is called when a portion of the file copy has completed.

DWORD WINAPI LP_SOUNDTRACK_PROGRESS_ROUTINE(
  PCWSTR pszSoundtrackName,
  PCWSTR pszSongName,
  LARGE_INTEGER TotalFileSize,
  LARGE_INTEGER TotalBytesTransferred,
  LPVOID Context
);

Parameters

pszSoundtrackName
[in] Pointer to a null-terminated string that specifies the name of the soundtrack.
pszSongName
[in] Pointer to a null-terminated string that specifies the name of the song.
TotalFileSize
[in] Value indicating the total file size in bytes.
TotalBytesTransferred
[in] Value indicating the total bytes transferred.
Context
[in] [optional] A user-defined value optionally supplied when calling XAddSongToSoundtrack.

Return Values

The LP_SOUNDTRACK_PROGRESS_ROUTINE function should return one of the following values:

Value Meaning
PROGRESS_CONTINUE Continue the copy operation.
PROGRESS_CANCEL Cancel the copy operation and delete the destination file.
PROGRESS_QUIET Continue the copy operation, but stop invoking LP_SOUNDTRACK_PROGRESS_ROUTINE to report progress.

Remarks

An application can use this information to display a progress bar that shows the total number of bytes copied as a percent of the total file size.

Requirements

Header: Declared in xbox.h; for include use xtl.h.

See Also

XAddSoundtrack, XAddSongToSoundtrack, XFindFirstSoundtrack, XFindNextSoundtrack, XGetSoundtrackSongInfo, XOpenSoundtrackSong
